fix(executor): init write_count=0 before loop to prevent UnboundLocalError

write_count was only assigned inside the loop body, causing UnboundLocalError
when force_tc condition checked 'write_count < 3' on round 0 before any
tool calls were processed.

commit.message:
feat(security): harden platform against arXiv:2602.20021 vulnerabilities

11 vulnerability classes addressed from red-teaming study of LLM agents:

## Adversarial guard (adversarial.py)
- PROMPT_INJECTION L0 (+8): detect instruction override in output/tool results [SBD-09]
- IDENTITY_CLAIM L0 (+7): detect agent impersonation ('I am Jarvis/admin/platform') [SBD-06]
- RESOURCE_ABUSE L0 (+7): detect DoS patterns in written code (busy-loops, fork bombs) [SBD-04]
- HARDCODED_SECRET: extend with JWT/Azure SAS/OpenAI key patterns [SBD-02]
- SECURITY_VULN: eval/exec/pickle/sql-injection/shell=True [SBD-08]
- FALSE_FALLBACK: stub/TODO detection in production code

## Tool scope (code_tools.py)
- _SENSITIVE_FILE_RE: block read/write/edit of .env, *.key, *.pem, SSH keys [SBD-02/SBD-08]
- audit_log on code_write (wires existing security/audit.py) [SBD-08]

## A2A bus (bus.py)
- from_agent identity validation: log+flag unregistered senders [SBD-06]
- Keeps existing _check_delegation_scope for SBD-01

## Memory sanitization (memory/manager.py)
- project_store + global_store: sanitize_agent_output() before INSERT [SBD-10/SBD-11]
- max 8K value cap to prevent memory DoS

## Executor budget (executor.py)
- MAX_TOOL_CALLS_PER_RUN=50 (env override) per-run hard limit [SBD-05]
- Raises BudgetExceededError on breach

## Documentation (CLAUDE.md)
- SECURITY section with arXiv:2602.20021 reference, threat table, Swiss Cheese model,
  intentional non-mitigations (Landlock, RBAC, A2A hard-block)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  - analyze_session(): 3 pattern types (bigram, dominant workflow, read-before-write)
  - upsert_instinct(): confidence tiers (0.3→0.9) with boosting on re-observation

  - Tables: instinct_observations, instincts (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() for PG compat)
  - ECC external module seeded in integrations table (Settings > Modules)

SOURCE: https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code
WHY: Instincts = atomic learned behaviors extracted from sessions -> skill evolution.

commit.message:
feat: add x402 seller HTTP server with paid API endpoints

Add an optional Express HTTP server that exposes MCP tools as x402
payment-gated endpoints. Other AI agents and clients can pay USDC to
query wallet data through standardized HTTP endpoints.

Endpoints:
  GET /api/usdc-balance       (0.001 USDC)
  GET /api/sol-balance        (0.001 USDC)
  GET /api/wallet-info        (0.001 USDC)
  GET /api/incoming-payments  (0.01 USDC)

New files:
  src/server.ts — Express app factory with @x402/express middleware

Config changes:
  X402_SERVER_PORT — opt-in: server only starts when this is set
  X402_FACILITATOR_URL — defaults to https://x402.org/facilitator

The MCP client is shared between the CLI REPL and HTTP server.
Server shuts down gracefully on SIGINT/SIGTERM alongside the CLI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add validate CLI command for .aiscrum/ config validation

Automated checks derived from 30 manual audit cycles:

1. files-exist: All instruction file paths resolve to existing files
2. prompts-exist: Role directories have expected prompt templates
3. phase-coverage: All ceremony phases used by sprint runner are configured
4. tool-policy: Instructions don't reference capabilities blocked by policy
   (e.g., shell commands in orchestrator/observer roles)
5. stakeholder-authority: All copilot-instructions.md include the section
6. template-vars: Prompt templates only use variables the ceremony provides
7. ghost-refs: No references to non-existent .aiscrum/ files

Usage: aiscrum validate [--path <project-dir>]

Exit code 1 if errors found, 0 if only warnings or clean.

Also: add quality-reviewer to CeremonyPhase type (was missing).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add interface{}/any overuse detection to antipattern analyzer

Implements Priority 4A.2 from ROADMAP.md: interface{}/any overuse detection.

Detection criteria:
- Flags functions with >2 any/interface{} parameters
- Flags functions with >30% any ratio (excludes single-param utilities)
- Detects both 'any' (Go 1.18+) and empty 'interface{}' types

Implementation details:
- Added checkAnyOveruse() to scan function signatures
- Extracted helper functions to maintain complexity <10:
  - countAnyUsage() - orchestrates counting
  - countAnyInParams() - counts any in parameters
  - countTotalParams() - counts total parameters
  - countAnyInResults() - counts any in results
  - exceedsAnyThreshold() - applies detection rules
  - isAnyType() - identifies any/interface{} types

Testing:
- 7 comprehensive test cases covering detection scenarios
- All tests pass with race detection
- Zero complexity regressions (all new functions ≤8 complexity)

Antipattern output includes:
- Type: any_overuse
- Severity: medium
- Suggestion: Use concrete types or constrained generics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Move flow creation from Azure Function to PA flow

Managed identity can't auth to Flow API (user-delegated only).
register-form now returns flow_create_body in the response.
The PA flow creates the data pipeline flow using an HTTP POST
to the Flow API with the user's own AD auth context.

Updated diagram and docs to show the 6-step flow.

commit.message:
fix: remove redundant master fetch in reset-uat workflow

The checkout action already checks out master with full history (fetch-depth: 0),
so fetching master:master is unnecessary and fails because Git refuses to fetch
into a branch that is already checked out.

commit.message:
Fix post date to avoid future-date filtering by Jekyll

Remove explicit 12:00 UTC time from front matter date so Jekyll
defaults to midnight, preventing the post from being filtered as
'future' when GitHub Pages builds before that time.

commit.message:
fix(lxc): add pulse user on OpenWrt host for D-Bus auth

PulseAudio inside the LXC container runs as uid 109 (pulse).
D-Bus EXTERNAL auth rejects connections from unknown uids, causing
module-bluez5-discover to fail and Bluetooth audio to not work.

Fix: create.sh now adds pulse:109 to the host /etc/passwd and
/etc/group. Also documented the issue in the OpenWrt README
known-issues section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: rename sp variable to sptr to avoid ARM64 stack pointer register conflict

The variable name 'sp' in http_url_decode and json_escape clashed with the
ARM64 stack pointer register, causing silent stack corruption. This broke
JSON key serialization (empty keys) and URL decoding on linux_arm64.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

highlight the following user-facing achievements:

improvements and memory efficiency.

fix: copy requirements.txt into test stage before installing requirements-dev.txt

requirements-dev.txt references '-r requirements.txt' so both files must
be present in the Docker build context before pip install runs in the
test stage. Previously only requirements-dev.txt was copied, causing
'No such file or directory: requirements.txt' in CI Docker builds.

fix(ac-qa): enforce write scope at executor level — reject non-QA writes

QA agents (role contains 'qa') can only code_write to:

- Dockerfile (for Chromium-only fixes)

commit.message:
refactor: defer daemon start until BT connected

Don't start sendspin daemon eagerly before Bluetooth connects.
Previously the daemon started on null-sink immediately, then
restarted when BT connected — causing a phantom player in MA
and unnecessary ClientRemovedEvent churn.

Now devices with bt_manager wait for actual BT connection before
spawning the daemon subprocess. Devices without BT (no mac) still
start immediately on default audio.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Wikidata image fallback via OTT→NCBI bridge for higher-order taxa

When OneZoom lacks images for higher-order taxa, falls back to Wikidata:
OTT → NCBI ID (tax_sources) → Wikidata SPARQL (P685→P18) → Wikimedia Commons

Fills last 7 missing images: zebra, camel, kangaroo, bat, beaver, sloth, monkey.
All 65 species now have complete data (name, scientific_name, ott_id, image_url).

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve clade display: collapsible sections, remove node names, add outside relatives

1. Remove confusing internal node names like "Laurasiatheria"
2. Make included animals section collapsible (closed by default)
3. Add "nearest relatives outside this group" section with distance
   labels, sorted by height above clade node, 20 per page with
   "show more" button

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add level-to-A and level-to-B labels in in-clade display, sort from animal 1 to animal 2

For each in-clade species, compute the tree distance (levels up to
common ancestor) to both selected animals. Sort species from animal 1
to animal 2 and display distance labels.

Co-authored-by: jacksonloper <2056977+jacksonloper@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix in-clade sorting with LCA-depth algorithm so A appears first and B last

Use depth(LCA(A,x)) descending / depth(LCA(B,x)) ascending to sort
species from animal A to animal B, per user's provided algorithm.
Also compute full tree distance for display labels.

commit.message:
fix: rename sp variable to sptr to avoid ARM64 stack pointer register conflict

The variable name 'sp' in strings.nov clashed with the ARM64 stack pointer
register, causing silent stack corruption in all string search/manipulation
functions including str_find, starts_with, ends_with, str_contains,
str_replace, str_trim, str_upper, str_lower, str_reverse, and str_is_blank.

This broke config file parsing, HTTP routing, and any code path using these
string operations. Renamed all 13 occurrences of 'sp' to 'sptr'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
